items.caption:
One of my favourite ways to help keep bugs away 🕷️✨

Recipe:
• 2 cups water
• 20–30 drops peppermint essential oil

Shake well before each use and spray around windows, doors, skirting boards, or directly onto your mop for larger areas.

I like to reapply every 1–2 weeks, especially during the warmer months.

#PeppermintOilHack #BugPrevention #HomeHacksWithCarolina #NaturalHomeTips #CleaningHacks

#cleaninghacks #bathroomcleaning #homehacks #deepclean #HomeHacksWithCarolina

Want your denim to last longer? 👖✨

• Wash separately or with similar colours to help prevent dye transfer.

• Turn denim inside out before washing to help reduce fading.

• Use cold water to help preserve the colour and reduce shrinking.

• Use vinegar instead of fabric softener in the rinse cycle to help remove detergent residue without coating the fabric.

• Don’t use too much detergent, as excess detergent can leave buildup behind.

• Air dry when possible to help maintain the fit and shape.

• If using a dryer, choose a low heat setting to help minimise shrinking.

• Avoid washing after every wear to help your denim last longer.

#DenimCare #LaundryTips #ClothingCare #LaundryHacks #DenimTips

The dusting trick I swear by ✨

Recipe:
• 1 cup water
• 1/4 cup white vinegar
• 1 tsp olive oil
• 10–15 drops lemon essential oil

Add everything to a spray bottle and shake well before each use.

I spray it directly onto surfaces, then wipe with a microfiber cloth. The vinegar helps remove residue, the oil helps reduce dust buildup, and the microfiber traps dust instead of spreading it around.

Repeat weekly to help keep dust under control.

#DustingTips #DustFreeHome #MicrofiberCleaning #CleaningHacks #HomeHacksWithCarolina

#LaundryHumor #Relatable #WashingMachine #LaundryLife #Adulting

Your couch collects more than you think 👀

Over time, upholstery traps dust, body oils and everyday dirt deep within the fabric. Steam helps loosen that buildup, refresh the fibres and reduce trapped odours without soaking the material.

I like to deep clean mine every 3–6 months, or more often if you have kids or pets at home ☕️

#CleaningTips #SteamCleaning #HomeHacksWithCarolina #CleanHome #DeepCleaning

Bath mats need a proper wash too ✨

I always wash mine separately, add baking soda to help remove odours and buildup, then add vinegar to the rinse cycle to help remove detergent residue. I also prefer washing them in warm water for a deeper clean without damaging the backing.

#CleaningTips #BathMatCleaning #HomeHacksWithCarolina #LaundryTips #CleanHome

Baking soda hacks you’ll actually use ✨

For drains: add baking soda and lemon juice, let it sit for 10–15 minutes, then rinse with hot water for a fresher smelling drain.

For fruit: soak in water with baking soda for 10–15 minutes to help remove dirt and pesticides.

For rugs and carpets: sprinkle baking soda over the surface, leave for 30 minutes, then vacuum as usual. Repeat monthly to keep them smelling fresh.

#CleaningTips #HomeHacksWithCarolina #BakingSodaHacks #KitchenHacks #CleanHome
